bestPractices.clearNamingDescription: Name contract lines in a way that clearly indicates their purpose and scope for this client.
bestPractices.clearNamingItem1: Include the contract line type in the name (e.g., "Monthly Support Bucket", "Project-Based Plan")
bestPractices.clearNamingItem2: Consider including dates or version numbers for contract lines that change over time
bestPractices.clearNamingItem3: Use consistent naming patterns across all contract lines for this client
bestPractices.description: Follow these best practices to ensure accurate billing and minimize confusion when managing multiple contract lines for this client.
bestPractices.documentRulesDescription: Clearly document how service overlaps should be handled for this specific client.
bestPractices.documentRulesItem1: Create client-specific guidelines for which contract line to use in different scenarios
bestPractices.documentRulesItem2: Share these guidelines with all team members who work with this client
bestPractices.documentRulesItem3: Include examples of common situations and how they should be handled
bestPractices.minimizeOverlapsDescription: Whenever possible, avoid having the same service in multiple contract lines for this client. This simplifies billing and reporting.
bestPractices.minimizeOverlapsItem1: Review the Service Overlap Matrix to identify overlapping services
bestPractices.minimizeOverlapsItem2: Consider consolidating contract lines or reorganizing services
bestPractices.minimizeOverlapsItem3: If overlaps are necessary, ensure clear documentation of which contract line should be used when
bestPractices.title: Best Practices for Contract Line Disambiguation

overview.benefitTracking: Simplified time entry and usage tracking
overview.bucketPriorityDescription: Bucket contract lines are given priority when disambiguating services for this client.
overview.description: When a client has multiple contract lines that include the same service, the system needs to determine which contract line to use for time entries and usage records. This guide explains how to manage this situation for this specific client.
overview.explicitAssignmentDescription: When a service appears in multiple contract lines, users must choose the intended assignment context instead of relying on implicit fallback.
overview.explicitSelectionDescription: When users must manually choose which contract line to bill against for this client.
overview.issueIncorrectBilling: Incorrect billing when assignment context is ambiguous
overview.issueReporting: Reporting inconsistencies across different contract lines
overview.issueUnexpectedBilling: Unexpected billing behavior for this client
overview.issueUserConfusion: User confusion when selecting contract lines for time entry
overview.serviceOverlapDescription: When the same service appears in multiple contract lines for this client.
overview.title: Understanding Contract Line Disambiguation for This Client

scenarios.description: These examples illustrate how contract line disambiguation works in common scenarios for this client.
scenarios.scenario1Description: When a service appears in both a bucket contract line and a standard contract line for this client:
scenarios.scenario1Item1: The bucket contract line is selected by default
scenarios.scenario1Item2: Time entries and usage consume the bucket's included amount, then continue on the same bucket contract line as overage at its configured overage rate
scenarios.scenario1Item3: Billing does not switch to the standard contract line automatically when the bucket is depleted
scenarios.scenario1Item4: Users can manually override this default by selecting a different contract line during time entry
scenarios.scenario2Description: When a service appears in multiple standard contract lines for this client:
scenarios.scenario2Item1: Users will be prompted to select which contract line to bill against during time entry
scenarios.scenario2Item2: If no contract line is explicitly selected, billing should stop with an ambiguity error that requires user choice
scenarios.scenario2Item3: Consider consolidating these contract lines to avoid confusion
scenarios.scenario3Description: When a service appears in multiple bucket contract lines for this client:
scenarios.scenario3Item1: Users will be prompted to select which bucket to bill against during time entry
scenarios.scenario3Item2: If no bucket is explicitly selected, billing should stop with an ambiguity error that requires user choice
scenarios.scenario3Item3: Teams can define internal guidance for choosing between overlapping buckets
scenarios.title: Common Disambiguation Scenarios

troubleshooting.description: Solutions for common issues related to contract line disambiguation for this client.
troubleshooting.issueReportingDescription: If you're seeing inconsistent reporting for services that appear in multiple contract lines for this client:
troubleshooting.issueReportingItem1: Use the contract_line_id filter in reports to see data for specific contract lines
troubleshooting.issueReportingItem2: Check if time entries or usage records have explicit contract line assignments
troubleshooting.issueReportingItem3: Review historical data to see if contract line assignments have changed over time
troubleshooting.issueReportingItem4: Consider updating the client's contract line configuration to reduce overlaps
troubleshooting.issueSelectionMissingDescription: If the contract line selection dropdown is not appearing during time entry for this client:
troubleshooting.issueSelectionMissingItem1: Verify that the service is actually included in multiple active contract lines
troubleshooting.issueSelectionMissingItem2: Check if one of the contract lines has expired or is not yet active
troubleshooting.issueSelectionMissingItem3: Ensure that the client and service selections are made before expecting the contract line dropdown
troubleshooting.issueSelectionMissingItem4: Try refreshing the page or clearing the browser cache
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanDescription: If time entries are being billed to the wrong contract line for this client:
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanItem1: Check if the time entry has an explicit contract_line_id assigned
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanItem2: Verify that the service is included in the expected contract line
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanItem3: Review the disambiguation rules to understand why a particular contract line was selected
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanItem4: Update the time entry to explicitly select the correct contract line
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanItem5: Consider updating the client's contract line configuration to avoid future issues
troubleshooting.issueWrongPlanTitle: Issue: Time Entry Billed to Wrong Plan
troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting Contract Line Disambiguation
